name: Annie Ross
abstract: Annie Ross (born Annabelle Allan Short; 25 July 1930 – 21 July 2020) was a British-born American singer and actress, best known as a member of the influential jazz vocal trio Lambert, Hendricks & Ross. She helped pioneer the vocalese style of jazz singing, with a style described by critic Dave Gelly as "a kind of dreamy watchfulness that…

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: Annie Ross and I got back together... creating words for big band arrangements... Annie Ross’ “Twisted” and “Farmer’s Market” which were done before Lambert, Hendricks & Ross
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: Even when he criticized Lambert, Hendricks, and Ross for the Duke Ellington album... One of them was Annie Ross... So I said to Dave and Annie... Lambert, Hendricks, and Ross
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Donn Andre
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy ended up with Lambert, Hendricks & Ross and the Ike Isaacs Trio, and now he works around New York, most recently he did a European tour with Annie Ross.
about_quotes.doc_title: Donn Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 6, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: So man, Lambert, Ross, and Hamilton. ... I met Annie Ross man when she was 11 years old ... me and Wig did a record ... we gave them all the money.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Kenny Clarke ... formed an orchestra, and I joined it. ... An English girl named Anabella Ross was the female vocalist.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Clarke
about_quotes.evidence: He said, 'Go up to the top, as far as you can get up in the balcony,' and it was like I was sitting right in front of the stage. And he told me. He said, -- well, Annie Ross was with us.
